_provenance.note: Transcribed verbatim; decision IDs assigned by array order (D-001..D-015), matching the gotcha.decisionId references in benchmark/tasks.ts and the repo README's decision table.

decisions.decision: DateRangePicker is the standard date range component. CalendarRange is deprecated after the mobile redesign and must not be used in new code.
decisions.rationale: CalendarRange had accessibility issues on mobile and was replaced during the mobile redesign. All new date-range UIs must use DateRangePicker from the design system.

decisions.decision: All data exports must go through the audit log middleware. Every export endpoint must call withAuditLog() before streaming data.
decisions.rationale: SOC-2 compliance requires a full audit trail of every data export. The audit log middleware captures who exported what, when, and the row count. Skipping it is a compliance violation.

decisions.decision: Primary buttons are reserved for data-mutating actions only. Read-only actions like exports, downloads, and navigation use the secondary button variant.
decisions.rationale: User testing showed decision fatigue when multiple primary buttons competed for attention. Reserving primary for mutations (save, delete, submit) creates a clear visual hierarchy.

decisions.decision: All list endpoints must use cursor-based pagination. Offset pagination is not allowed for new endpoints.
decisions.rationale: Offset pagination breaks when data is inserted or deleted during paging, causing duplicates or skipped records. Cursor pagination is stable and performs better on large datasets.

decisions.decision: Use the ShimmerSkeleton loading pattern from the design system. PulseLoader is deprecated and must not be used in new code.
decisions.rationale: Design system standardization effort unified all loading states to shimmer skeletons. They provide better perceived performance by showing content shape while loading.

decisions.decision: The authentication middleware is frozen and must not be modified. Any auth changes require a compliance review first.
decisions.rationale: The auth middleware passed a compliance audit (SEC-2024-0915-AUTH). Any modification requires re-certification. Changes must go through the security team and compliance review process.

decisions.decision: All user-facing error messages must include a link to the relevant help center article. Use the helpCenterUrl() utility to generate links.
decisions.rationale: Support ticket analysis showed 40% of tickets were about errors with clear help center documentation. Adding links directly in error messages reduced repeat tickets significantly.

decisions.decision: Feature flags must use posthog.isFeatureEnabled() from the PostHog SDK. Custom feature flag implementations are not allowed.
decisions.rationale: PostHog provides centralized flag management, gradual rollouts, and analytics integration. Custom implementations fragment flag state and lack audit trails.

decisions.decision: Test files must be co-located next to their source files (e.g., utils.test.ts next to utils.ts). Do not use a separate __tests__/ directory.
decisions.rationale: Co-location makes it obvious when a file lacks tests and simplifies imports. The __tests__/ convention caused files to drift out of sync with their sources.

decisions.decision: All API routes must use Zod schemas for request validation wrapped in the withAuth middleware. This is the standard pattern for all new API endpoints.
decisions.rationale: Zod provides runtime type safety with TypeScript inference. The withAuth wrapper handles authentication, rate limiting, and error formatting consistently.

decisions.decision: The notification system uses async digest delivery. Real-time push notifications are a deliberate non-goal for the current product phase.
decisions.rationale: User research showed notification fatigue with real-time alerts. Async digests let users batch-process notifications at their own pace, improving engagement metrics. Real-time was evaluated and rejected per ADR-007.

decisions.decision: All list views must support full keyboard navigation including arrow keys, Enter to select, and Escape to deselect. This is an accessibility requirement.
decisions.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requires keyboard operability for all interactive elements. List views are a core interaction pattern and must be navigable without a mouse.

decisions.decision: All database queries must use the Drizzle query builder. Raw SQL queries are not allowed in application code.
decisions.rationale: Drizzle provides type-safe queries, automatic migration generation, and protection against SQL injection. Raw SQL bypasses these safeguards and makes refactoring harder.

decisions.decision: Environment configuration must use @t3-oss/env-nextjs for validation. Direct process.env access is not allowed -- all env vars must be declared in the env schema.
decisions.rationale: T3 env provides build-time validation of required environment variables and TypeScript types. Direct process.env access is untyped and fails silently when vars are missing.

decisions.decision: Toast notifications must use the sonner library. Do not use react-hot-toast, custom toast implementations, or window.alert.
decisions.rationale: Sonner integrates with our design system tokens, supports accessible announcements, and provides consistent animations. Multiple toast libraries create inconsistent UX.
